tieude: INTERN RECRUITMENT

mieutachitiet:

- Receive trainees and consult on admission to courses/classes

- Create content and design media publications for courses/classes

- Participate and support the organization of conferences, seminars, courses/classes

  • Proficient in Word and Excel

Personal CV (Candidate's profile information is kept strictly confidential).

tieude: 2024 TRAINING AND SKILL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM FOR GRASSROOTS TRADE UNION OFFICERS IN KHANH HOA PROVINCE
mieutangan: The Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation, in collaboration with the Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development, organized a training program for nearly 100 grassroots trade union officers.
mieutachitiet:
On June 26, 2024, at the Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute), in cooperation with the Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation, organized a training and skill development course for nearly 100 trade union officers, including Chairperson, Vice Chairpersons, and officials from district-level labor federations and equivalent industry trade unions.

The opening ceremony of the training course was attended by Comrade Bui Dang Thanh, Standing Vice President of the Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation, along with other members of the Federation's Standing Committee, and nearly 100 trade union officers.


Mr. Bui Dang Thanh, Standing Vice President of the Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation, delivering the opening speech.
(Photos: Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation)

Dr. To Nhi A, a psychologist representing the Phuong Nam Institute, delivered three key presentations on:

1. Employee Motivation and Inspiration;
2. Insights into Workers' Psychology;
3. Achieving Work-Life Balance.


Dr. To Nhi A - Psychologist. (Photo: Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation)

“This training course aims to equip grassroots trade union officers with knowledge, skills enhancement, and methods of union activities, contributing to the development of a workforce in capable of meeting the challenges in the new context.” – said Comrade Bui Dang Thanh, Standing Vice President of the Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation.


Grassroots trade union officers from Khanh Hoa Province actively engaging in the training session. 


Grassroots trade union officers posing for a commemorative photo with the organizing committee.
(Photo: Khanh Hoa Province Labor Federation)

 

By Dinh Duong.

tieude: TRAINING AND SKILL DEVELOPMENT IN CUSTOMER CARE & NEGOTIATION SKILLS FOR BIDV PHO NUI - GIA LAI BRANCH
tieudeseo: TRAINING AND SKILL DEVELOPMENT IN CUSTOMER CARE & NEGOTIATION SKILLS FOR BIDV PHO NUI - GIA LAI BRANCH
mieutangan: Accompanying more than 60 staff members of BIDV Pho Nui in training and skill development from June 29 to June 30, 2024.
mieutachitiet:
From June 29 to June 30, 2024, at the BIDV Pho Nui Branch in Gia Lai Province, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) organized a training and skill development course for over 60 officers, staff members, and leaders of various departments and divisions at BIDV Pho Nui.

On behalf of the BIDV Pho Nui Branch in Gia Lai Province, Mr. Trao An Qui, the Deputy Director of the branch, was present to represent the bank's leadership and deliver the opening speech.


Mr. Trao An Qui, Deputy Director of the Branch, giving the opening speech
(Photos: BIDV Pho Nui Bank)

Representing Phuong Nam Institute, Dr. To Nhi A, a member of the Scientific Advisory Council of the Southern Institute and also the reporter of the training course, was in attendance. 


Dr. To Nhi A - Psychologist (Photos: BIDV Pho Nui Bank)

In the two-day training course, Dr. To Nhi A, a psychologist, delivered two key sessions on "Customer Care Skills" and "Negotiation and Bargaining Skills." These sessions were conducted using practical, real-world approaches, designed to equip officers and staff working in the banking sector with valuable, actionable skills.


The training and skill development session being lively and engaging, featuring interactive activities


Two days of training, filled with practical insights and a sense of excitement
(Photos: BIDV Pho Nui Bank)

Phuong Nam Institute hopes that "Through these two days of learning, we hope that Dr. To Nhi A, together with all BIDV’s officers and staff members, will have the opportunity to access, share, and exchange valuable information, achieving the desired outcomes. This will contribute to BIDV's message of “Moving forward, growing together” — which reflects BIDV's position, commitment, and development goals. This slogan embodies a profound meaning and message that BIDV wishes to convey, serving as a guiding principle for all of the bank's activities to successfully achieve its goal of becoming the leading bank in Vietnam and the region, while contributing to the overall development of the country.”

By Dinh Duong

tieude: BAC LIEU PROVINCE LABOR FEDERATION ENHANCES THE QUALITY OF GRASSROOTS TRADE UNIONS
tieudeseo: THE BAC LIEU PROVINCE LABOR FEDERATION ENHANCES THE QUALITY OF GRASSROOTS TRADE UNIONS
mieutangan: In order to effectively carry out the Resolution of the 11th Congress of Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, a key focus should be placed on training and enhancing the professional expertise of trade union officials, making it a top priority.
mieutachitiet:
On September 5, 2024, in line with the 2024 working program of the Standing Committee of Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, at the conference hall of Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, the provincial Standing Committee, in cooperation with Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) in  Ho Chi Minh City, organized a professional training course for trade union officers in 2024, attended by 70 key grassroots trade union officers.

The opening ceremony was attended by Comrade Huynh Hoang Anh, Vice President of Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, along with the heads and deputy heads of the specialized committees.

Representing Phuong Nam Institute, the event was attended by Mr. Nguyen Tat Nam, LL.M., a member of the Institute's Advisory Council, and Mr. Duong Ngoc Minh Triet, M.Sc., Director of the Center for Training and Science-Technology Services.

To successfully implement the Resolution of the 11th Congress of Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, right from the beginning of the term, Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation proactively advised the Executive Committee to implement various tasks, with a focus on training, professional development, and skills-building. These efforts aim to enhance the management capacity and confidence of grassroots trade union officers, enabling them to effectively respond to new challenges in line with the objectives set forth in the Resolution.

Over the course of two days, September 5 and 6, 2024, the presenters delivered four key topics: 1. Effective negotiation and dialogue skills to enhance the quality of collective labor agreements; 2. Communication skills for union activities on the internet; 3. Skills in writing news articles and taking photos for websites and social media; 4. Trade union involvement in workplace safety management. After the two-day training, participants evaluated the course based on several criteria: Over 90% agreed that the training content met the essential needs of trade union officers’ activities; more than 95% found the presentation style clear, easy to understand, and professional; over 92% reported that they had gained a good understanding of the content, reflecting high quality; additionally, more than 95% of participants praised the organization and service attitude of Phuong Nam Institute, with the same percentage expressing that the course duration was appropriate and well-suited to the training goals.

 Discussion, practice, and presentation groups

After two days of working with urgency, seriousness, and responsibility, the professional training course for trade union officers has successfully completed all the planned content and program. Following this training, participants have listened to the presentations of Phuong Nam Institute's trainers on four key topics related to advocacy skills, dialogue, and negotiation. These are specialized skills that are highly practical and beneficial, which will enable trade union officers to apply them effectively in their union activities at their respective workplaces." - Comrade Huynh Nguyen Truc Linh, Standing Vice President of  Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation, remarked at the closing ceremony.

Comrade Huynh Nguyen Truc Linh, Standing Vice President of  Bac Lieu Province Labor Federation

By Dinh Duong

tieude: TRAINING CONSULTANT RECRUITMENT
tieudeseo: Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development is recruiting a Training Consultant to perform the functions of the Institute.
mieutangan: Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) is recruiting a Training Consultant to perform the functions of the Institute.
mieutachitiet:
I. Job Description:

1. Expected Outcomes: 

  • Recruit students for each course/class.
  • Manage courses/classes smoothly and efficiently.

2. Specific Tasks:

  • Master the content framework and detailed program of each course/class.
  • Identify the scope for looking for learner sources and target learners.
  • Proactively seek potential trainees and store relevant information.
  • Based on available data, actively contact learners and provide them with essential information about the course/class.
  • Address any questions or concerns from trainees.

  •   Ability to receive, convey, negotiate, and persuade information; 
  •   Good listening and understanding skills;
  •   Proficient in Microsoft Office;
  •   Ability to plan, organize, and arrange work systematically and harmoniously;

  •   Polite and sociable demeanor.
  •   Able to work under pressure.
  •   Calm, patient, honest, sincere, creative, with a spirit of sharing and solidarity.
  •   Proactive in work.
  •   Eager to learn for personal development.
  •   Enthusiastic, with a long-term commitment to the Institute.
  •   Quick, flexible, and professional working style.

III. Benefits:

- Salary negotiable (depending on competence).

tieude: New Sci-tech Law to remove ’bottlenecks’ in commercialising research results
tieudeseo: New Sci-tech Law to remove ’bottlenecks’ in commercialising research results
mieutangan: The 2013 Law on Science and Technology will be amended and renamed the Law on Science, Technology and Innovation, helping to remove institutional obstacles and promote research results.
mieutachitiet:
Deputy Minister of Science and Technology Bùi Thế Duy. — Photo vnexpress.net

The 2013 Law on Science and Technology will be amended and renamed the Law on Science, Technology and Innovation, helping to remove institutional obstacles and promote research results to the market, according to Deputy Minister of Science and Technology Bùi Thế Duy.

Duy said that the amended 2013 Law on Science and Technology will be submitted to the National Assembly for consideration and comments in May 2025.

According to the official, one of the 'bottlenecks' that makes it difficult for research results to be translated into practice is related to the legal framework. In particular, the law on science and technology and many related laws do not accept the fact that sometimes scientists do research without producing results. This leads to the fact that when registering tasks, scientists do not dare to register new content, mainly creating content at a safe level so it is not new and cannot be brought to life.

“Besides, scientific and technological results also take a long time, 10-20 years, to be brought to life, so if we only evaluate the results of commercialisation in one to five years, it is not complete,” said Duy.

The second reason is due to the separation between research-development and production-business sectors.

Duy said that the 2013 Law on Science and Technology mentioned the issue of ordering research, but the ordering must be from businesses or associated with universities and businesses must be through staff exchange activities. Currently, the staff exchange corridor is not favourable or a mandatory activity for lecturers and researchers.

"The proposed revised law suggests introducing a corridor for scientists to work with businesses, which are still considered research and teaching activities," Duy said.

Clearly defining the stages in research and development activities and innovation activities is also an issue of concern in amending the law. Currently, the fact that university research must be transferred to businesses is not practical. Therefore, the revised law needs to promote experimental production in businesses and analyse the market to produce final products.

"The amended law will create a corridor for intermediary organisations to protect intellectual property and form a new science and technology market to facilitate the transfer of results," said the deputy minister.

He also suggested that national programmes need to have a mechanism to assign results to the most favourable host organisation, considering science and technology investment as a long-term investment, automatically assigning authority for the lead agency to continue using it for commercialisation and further investment to create new products.

In the proposals to amend the Law on Science and Technology, Deputy Minister Duy also emphasised new groups of policy issues. Accordingly, to develop science and technology activities in universities, it is necessary to have research content, activities and investment funds for the university. For example, there needs to be a programme to train graduate students with funding from science and technology.

Duy said that many countries around the world consider graduate students as the main research force because they are young, creative and passionate about their work. Therefore, it is necessary to develop training programmes, considering them as researchers and research workers, not just students.

A Viettel engineer doing some research. — Photo vnexpress.net

Prof. Dr. Nguyễn Hữu Đức, Hanoi National University, expects that the amended law will create a new legal corridor, creating conditions for development and encouraging innovation.

"This is the first time we have formalised the concept of innovation, which will help build a new culture for Vietnamese people," Đức said.

Hanoi National University currently focuses on investing in creative student startups. According to Professor Đức, it is necessary to prepare human resources for innovation, adding subjects on inventive and creative entrepreneurship into general subjects so that students have the skills and capacity to be pioneers.

Shortcomings in financial investment mechanisms for science and technology

Currently, the financial investment mechanism for science and technology still has many shortcomings. Deputy Minister Duy said that the Ministry of Science and Technology has researched and evaluated the entire legal system, including many related laws. The reality is that there is a lack of synchronisation between laws on science and technology and laws on finance.

“Once there are investment funds and resources, they need to be used effectively, openly and quickly. To achieve this, the science and technology industry needs to strongly amend regulations related to science and technology tasks, topics, projects, decentralisation and reform of administrative procedures," said Duy.

“The goal is to be open and transparent, thereby selecting the best topics and tasks quickly and conveniently.”

The first thing that needs to be resolved is how to synchronise the provisions of the Law on Science and Technology with existing financial policies, to make the most of all of these policies.

In addition, it is necessary to adjust regulations on procurement and bidding when using investment funds for technology, including public investment funds from the State and funds from enterprises. This will encourage State support mechanisms.

"It is necessary to research new issues, core technologies and even purchase research results and inventions from abroad, then transfer them to businesses so they can use and develop", said Duy.

To "find a common voice between managers, financial investors and scientists", the Deputy Minister believes that it is necessary to share the view that State funding for science and technology results should be considered a long-term investment.

Instead of requiring immediate payment, funds should be assigned to the host units and production units so they can invest and develop production and business. When these units create jobs and pay taxes, the State will recover capital through taxes and reinvest in scientific and technological activities.

To achieve this, there needs to be close cooperation between science and technology agencies and financial agencies to understand each other and coordinate effectively.

Therefore, the ministry proposes that the Law will regulate the determination of what is property and what is not property in scientific and technological results. For example, results that have been widely published and become human knowledge cannot be considered 'private property'.

"We believe that sharing this perspective will help adjust the legal corridor of the Law on Science and Technology as well as other laws, so that they become more appropriate and operate more effectively, thereby creating an open environment for science, technology and innovation activities," emphasised Duy. — VNS

Source: https://vietnamnet.vn/en/new-sci-tech-law-to-remove-bottlenecks-in-commercialising-research-results-2309033.html

tieude: Khoá học về “Phương Pháp Nghiên Cứu Khoa Học và Công Bố Quốc Tế”
tieudeseo: Khoá học về “Phương Pháp Nghiên Cứu Khoa Học và Công Bố Quốc Tế”
mieutangan: Tôi hân hạnh giới thiệu một khoá học 7 ngày (từ 16/12/2024 đến 22/12/2024) do tôi và TS Trần Sơn Thạch sẽ phụ trách tại Viện Phương Nam. Chi tiết về mục tiêu và chương trình học như sau:
mieutachitiet:
Tôi hân hạnh giới thiệu một khoá học 7 ngày (từ 16/12/2024 đến 22/12/2024) do tôi và TS Trần Sơn Thạch sẽ phụ trách tại Viện Phương Nam. Chi tiết về mục tiêu và chương trình học như sau:

https://vienphuongnam.com.vn/khoa-hoc-chuyen-gia-phuong-phap-phan-tich-du-lieu-va-cong-bo-quoc-te

Bối cảnh

Nghiên cứu khoa học là một cách kiến tạo tri thức và chứng cớ làm cơ sở cho chánh sách công. Trong y tế, kết quả của nghiên cứu khoa học làm chứng cớ cho thực hành lâm sàng và xây dựng chành sách y tế công cộng. Tuy nhiên, trong thực tế ở nước ta, nghiên cứu khoa học còn rất nhiều bất cập vì vấn đề phương pháp. Ở vài nơi, công bố khoa học cũng là một tiêu chuẩn cứng trong việc học hành và sự nghiệp khoa học. Thế nhưng đa số các nghiên cứu ở Việt Nam chưa/không được công bố, hoặc có công bố nhưng trên những tập san có chất lượng thấp.

Một khó khăn lớn đối với các đồng nghiệp là vấn đề viết bài báo khoa học và chọn tập san thích hợp. Viết bài báo khoa học bằng tiếng Anh là một thách thức lớn, nên vài người nghĩ rằng có thể dùng chatGPT viết, nhưng đó là một suy nghĩ rất sai. Vì chưa am hiểu văn hoá công bố khoa học nên nhiều đồng nghiệp chọn sai tập san, thậm chí rơi vào bẫy của các tập san dỏm.

Tất cả những khó khăn này làm cho tình trạng công bố khoa học của Việt Nam tụt hậu so với các nước trong vùng. Để góp phần vào việc nâng cao năng lực công bố khoa học, chúng tôi tổ chức một lớp tập huấn 7 ngày về phương pháp nghiên cứu và công bố bài báo khoa học.

Mục tiêu

Khoá học có mục tiêu chánh là trang bị cho học viên kĩ năng và kiến thức về thiết kế nghiên cứu khoa học. Một mục tiêu khác là giúp cho học viên biết cách soạn và công bố bài báo khoa học trên các tập san quốc tế. Chúng tôi kì vọng rằng sau khi hoàn tất khóa học, học viên sẽ:

  • biết được cách phát biểu và đánh giá câu hỏi nghiên cứu;
  • thiết kế bộ câu hỏi cho thu thập dữ liệu;
  • xây dựng đề cương nghiên cứu;
  • nguyên tắc phân tích dữ liệu;
  • cấu trúc IMRaD của một bài báo khoa học; 
  • nguyên tắc trong cách viết phần Dẫn nhập, Phương pháp, Kết quả, Bàn luận, và cách đặt nhan đề bài báo; 
  • biết cách soạn biểu đồ và bảng số liệu cho bài báo khoa học; 
  • làm quen với văn phong khoa học và tiếng Anh trong khoa học;
  • biết cách ứng dụng ChatGPT trong biên tập bài báo khoa học;
  • phân biệt tập san chánh thống và tập san dỏm.

Học viên

Lớp học được thiết kế dành cho các sinh viên sau đại học, nghiên cứu sinh, giảng viên, nhà khoa học có nhu cầu nghiên cứu và công bố quốc tế. Học viên cần có trình đột tiếng Anh có thể viết một câu văn tương đối hoàn chỉnh (không cần có trình độ tiếng Anh cao).

Giảng viên

Tôi (Nguyễn Văn Tuấn) và TS Trần Sơn Thạch.

Chúng tôi, ngoài làm nghiên cứu khoa học, còn phục vụ trong các hội đồng biên tập các tập san khoa học và tư vấn cho các tập đoàn dược quốc tế. Trong những vai trò đó, chúng tôi có kinh nghiệm lâu năm về quản lí khoa học, thiết kế nghiên cứu, phân tích dữ liệu, và công bố khoa học. Chúng tôi có thể chia sẻ những vấn đề rất thực tế với các đồng nghiệp trong nước. Chúng tôi đã giúp cho hàng ngàn đồng nghiệp trong suốt 25 năm qua.

Địa điểm

Viện phát triển nguồn lực xã hội Phương Nam

Số 45 Đinh Tiên Hoàng, Phường Bến Nghé, Quận 1, TPHCM.

Thời gian

Ngày khai giảng: 8h00 ngày 16/12/2024 (thứ 2)

Thời lượng khóa học: 7 ngày (từ 16/12 đến 22/12/2024)

Hình thức học chính: Trực tiếp/offline; có hỗ trợ trực tuyến/online cho những học viên có nhu cầu.

Thời gian học: Buổi sáng từ 8h00 – 11h30, Buổi chiều từ 13h00 – 17h00.

Liên lạc ghi danh

Mọi thắc mắc về khóa học, xin vui lòng liên lạc:

Hotline: (028) 2242 6789 

Ms. Vy: 0949 761 636 

Mr. Triết: 0902 315 531

Nội dung

Nội dung gồm 23 bài giảng, đưa chia thành 2 phần: thiết kế nghiên cứu và cách viết bài báo khoa học.

Phần I: Thiết kế nghiên cứu

Bài giảng 1: Qui trình nghiên cứu khoa học. Giới thiệu những bước cần thiết trong việc thiết kế một công trình nghiên cứu khoa học, từ phát biểu giả thuyết, thiết kế thí nghiệm / nghiên cứu, đến công bố khoa học. 

Bài giảng 2: Câu hỏi nghiên cứu. Phát biểu câu hỏi nghiên cứu rất quan trọng vì một câu hỏi rõ ràng giúp cho nghiên cứu có cơ may thành công cao. Chúng tôi sẽ giới thiệu công thức PICOT và cách đánh giá câu hỏi nghiên cứu theo công thức FINER.

Bài giảng 3: Mô hình nghiên cứu: thử nghiệm.  Sau câu hỏi nghiên cứu là chọn mô hình nghiên cứu. Bài giảng này sẽ giới thiệu triết lí và mô hình thử nghiệm (randomized trial) rất phổ biến trong y khoa nhưng cũng áp dụng trong giáo dục và khoa học xã hội.

Bài giảng 4: Mô hình nghiên cứu: quan sát. Bài giảng sẽ tập trung vào các mô hình nghiên cứu quan sát như nghiên cứu đoàn hệ, nghiên cứu ‘bệnh-chứng’, nghiên cứu cắt ngang, v.v. Những điểm mạnh và yếu của từng mô hình sẽ được thảo luận.

Bài giảng 5: Đề cương nghiên cứu. Đề cương nghiên cứu là một văn bản kế hoạch hoá nghiên cứu. Bài giảng này sẽ hướng dẫn cách viết một đề cương nghiên cứu sao cho thuyết phục.

Bài giảng 6: Sai lệch và nhiễu trong nghiên cứu khoa học. Các nghiên cứu quan sát thường bị chi phối bởi các yếu tố lệch (biases) và nhiễu (confounders). Bài giảng sẽ điểm qua những khái niệm và minh hoạ bằng những ví dụ thực tế.

Bài giảng 7: Ước tính cỡ mẫu. Số lượng đối tượng cần thiết cho một nghiên cứu quyết định sự thành bại của một công trình nghiên cứu. Bài giảng sẽ điểm qua và mô tả những phương pháp chuẩn để ước tính cỡ mẫu cho mỗi mô hình nghiên cứu.

Bài giảng 8: Phương pháp soạn bộ câu hỏi. Bộ câu hỏi (questionnaire) là một phương tiện để thu thập dữ liệu. Dữ liệu phải bảo đảm tính hợp lí nội tại (internal validity). Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu các nguyên tắc trong việc soạn câu hỏi và câu trả lời.

Bài giảng 9: Nguyên tắc phân tích dữ liệu. Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu 5 nguyên tắc trong phân tích dữ liệu và soạn biểu đồ.

Bài giảng 10: Phương pháp phân tích dữ liệu: mô tả. Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu các phương pháp phân tích mô tả như tính toán khoảng tin cậy 95%, t-test, Chi-squared test, hệ số tương quan.

Bài giảng 11: Phương pháp phân tích dữ liệu: suy luận. Tiếp theo bài giảng 10 là những phương pháp phân tích suy luận (inferential analysis). Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu các mô hình hồi qui tuyến tính, hồi qui logistic, và mô hình tiên lượng.

Phần II: Cách soạn bài báo khoa học và công bố

Bài giảng 12: Cấu trúc IMRaD.

Bài giảng 13: Cách đặt nhan đề (title) bài báo. Nhan đề bài báo khoa học là một yếu tố rất quan trọng, có khi quyết định sự thành bại của bài báo, nhưng rất ít được các tác giả chú ý. Bài giảng này sẽ giới thiệu những nguyên tắc trong việc đặt tựa đề, cùng những điều không nên làm khi đặt nhan đề. Một số nghiên cứu khoa học về tựa đề bài báo cũng sẽ được trình bày để minh họa cho các nguyên tắc chung.

Bài giảng 14: Cách viết abstract. Bản tóm tắt(abstract) bài báo khoa học đóng vai trò quan trọng vì đa số ban biên tập chỉ có thời gian đọc abstract, nhưng đa số tác giả viết không tốt. Bài giảng này sẽ giới thiệu 5 nguyên tắc cần thiết để viết một abstract cho thuyết phục.

Bài giảng 15: Cách viết phần dẫn nhập. Phần Dẫn nhập của một bài báo khoa học là lí do tồn tại của bài báo, nên cần phải được quan tâm đúng mức. Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu công thức viết dẫn nhập có tên là CaRS (creating a research space). Bài giảng cũng sẽ giới thiệu những câu văn quen thuộc để giúp các bạn viết bài báo lần đầu.

Bài giảng 16: Cách viết phần phương pháp. Bài giảng sẽ giới thiệu những phần thông tin liên quan đến thiết kế nghiên cứu, đối tượng nghiên cứu, phương pháp đo lường, phương pháp phân tích, v.v. cho một bài báo y học. Mỗi mục sẽ được minh họa bằng những câu văn quen thuộc hay những mô tả đã được công bố trên các tập san y học nổi tiếng trên thế giới.

Bài giảng 17: Cách soạn bảng biểu. Bải giảng sẽ hướng dẫn cách thiết kế bảng số liệu và những biểu đồ có phẩm chất cao.

Bài giảng 18: Cách viết phần kết quả. Kết quả là trái tim của một công trình nghiên cứu. Nhưng viết phần Kết quả có khi là một thách thức đáng kể cho những người mới vào nghiên cứu, vì không biết viết cái gì trước và cái gì sau, hay viết sao cho thuyết phục. Bài giảng này sẽ trình bày một số phương pháp và nguyên tắc quan trọng trong việc mô tả kết quả nghiên cứu. Phần đầu sẽ bàn về cách viết.

Bài giảng 19: Cách viết phần bàn luận. Đây là phần khó viết nhất trong một bài báo khoa học. Các sách hướng dẫn viết bài báo khoa học bằng tiếng Anh ít khi bàn đến phần này một cách cụ thể. Ngay cả những người ở cấp giáo sư vẫn viết phần bàn luận chưa đạt. Trong bài giảng này, các bạn sẽ học một công thức đơn giản (gồm 6 đoạn văn) nhưng rất hiệu quả trong việc cấu trúc phần bàn luận.

Bài giảng 20: Sử dụng chatGPT. Thời gian gần đây, ChatGPT xuất hiện và làm thay đổi cách soạn bài báo khoa học, dẫn đến nhiều lạm dụng. Bài giảng này sẽ bàn về ứng dụng ChatGPT trong việc viết một bài báo khoa học.

Bài giảng 21: Trả lời bình duyệt. Sau khi bài báo được gởi đi, nếu may mắn sẽ được gởi ra ngòai bình duyệt (peer review) bởi các chuyên gia. Nhiệm vụ của tác giả là trả lời những bình luận, phê bình, câu hỏi của chuyên gia bình duyệt. Trả lời bình duyệt vừa là một khoa học vừa là một nghệ thuật. Bài giảng này sẽ hướng dẫn các bạn cách trả lời cụ thể mà các tập san y khoa ưa chuộng.

Bài giảng 22: Chọn tập san khoa học. Chọn tập san thích hợp để công bố kết quả nghiên cứu đang là một vấn đề thời sự, vì có quá nhiều tập san “dỏm” trên thế giới. Điều nguy hiểm hơn nữa là có những tập san nằm ở biên giới dỏm và thật. Rất nhiều nhà khoa học Việt Nam đã trở thành nạn nhân của những tập san dỏm. Bài giảng này sẽ giới thiệu các tiêu chuẩn để giúp các bạn phân biệt tập san dỏm và tập san thật, cùng những tiêu chí để chọn tập san chuyên ngành thích hợp cho nghiên cứu.

Bài giảng 23: Tiếng Anh trong bài báo khoa học. Một phần lớn bài giảng sẽ xoay quanh vấn đề tiếng Anh (như văn phong và cách chọn từ ngữ) trong bài báo khoa học. Nguyên tắc IDEA sẽ được giới thiệu và lí giải trong cách viết bài báo khoa học.

Tài liệu tham khảo

  • Cẩm nang nghiên cứu khoa học của Nguyễn Văn Tuấn, Nhà xuất bản Tổng Hợp TPHCM.
  • Từ nghiên cứu đến công bố: kĩ năng mềm cho nhà khoa học của Nguyễn Văn Tuấn, Nhà xuất bản Tổng Hợp TPHCM.
  • Đi vào nghiên cứu khoa học của Nguyễn Văn Tuấn, Nhà xuất bản Tổng Hợp TPHCM.

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE OFFICIALLY ORGANIZED THE OPENING CEREMONY FOR THE EXPERT COURSE “RESEARCH METHODOLOGY AND SCIENTIFIC PUBLICATION”
mieutangan: On December 16, 2024, Phuong Nam Institute held the opening ceremony for the expert course "Research methodology and scientific publication".
mieutachitiet:
Mission to elevate the quality of research methodology and scientific publication.

This morning, Phuong Nam Institute held the opening ceremony of the expert course "Research methodology and scientific publication", with Professor Nguyen Van Tuan as the instructor. This is a special course that lays the foundation for an advanced training program, aimed at providing medical staff and scientists nationwide with essential knowledge and skills.

Overview of the opening ceremony

The course takes place from December 16 to December 22, 2024 at 45 Dinh Tien Hoang, Ben Nghe Ward, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City. The goal is to equip participants with the skills and knowledge necessary for designing scientific research. Additionally, the course will guide participants how to design and publish scientific articles in international journals.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan gave a speech at the opening ceremony

The expert course has attracted nearly 200 participants, including experts, medical staff, and researchers from over 50 organizations nationwide. This large turnout reflects the urgent need among individuals and organizations to enhance their scientific research capabilities as well as their ability to publish internationally in Vietnam.

Providing society with developed resources enhanced to international standards

In the context of integration and sustainable development, training high-quality human resources is a decisive factor for socio-economic progress. Therefore, improving the quality of education and training of human resources has become a guideline in the construction and development goals of Phuong Nam Institute.

In the opening ceremony, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, the Director of Phuong Nam Institute, emphasized the importance of developing well-structured, specialized training programs, particularly in the face of increasing international integration. Through the course, the Institute hopes to help students access advanced research methods, improve the quality of scientific works and promote publication in prestigious international journals.

Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy delivered the opening speech

In addition, the expert course is not only a bridge connecting organizations and partners but also an environment for scientists and learners at home and abroad to share knowledge and experience in research. At the same time, it promotes cooperation to explore potential and enhance their careers. This contributes to creating a close cooperation network, towards the sustainable development of the country's science.

The expert course "Research methodology and scientific publication" is a solid foundation in building the following courses and affirming the pioneering role of Phuong Nam Institute in training high-quality human resources not only in the field of scientific research, international integration but also in many other fields.

tieude: SUMMARY OF THE EXPERT COURSE "RESEARCH METHODOLOGY AND SCIENTIFIC PUBLICATION"
mieutangan: On the afternoon of December 22, 2024, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development organized the closing ceremony, officially concluding the 7-day expert course "Research Methodology and Scientific Publication."
mieutachitiet:
The course attracted nearly 200 participants from 104 educational, healthcare, and business organizations across 18 provinces and cities nationwide. This event not only emphasized the importance of enhancing the quality of scientific research and international publication but also demonstrated Phuong Nam Institute's dedication to promoting the development of scientific research in Vietnam.

The closing ceremony was honored by the presence of distinguished guests, including:

1. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Phuoc - Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Union of Science and Technology Associations, Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Scientific Council, Chairman of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board;

2. Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute;

3. Dr. Nguyen Trung Dong, meritorious artist, President of Institute for Public Policy and Rural Development, Member of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board;

4. Mr. Do Van Muoi - Honorary Consul of the Republic of Estonia in Ho Chi Minh City, Member of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board;

5. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan, Director of the Centre for Health Technologies at the University of Technology Sydney;

6. Dr. Tran Son Thach, University of Technology Sydney.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan, Prof. Nguyen Van Phuoc, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, and Mr. Do Van Muoi presented the certificates and took commemorative photos with the participants.

The curriculum consists of 11 lectures on research design and 12 lectures on writing scientific papers and publishing internationally. Despite the time constraints, with the dedicated teaching of Professor Nguyen Van Tuan and Teaching Assistant Dr. Tran Son Thach, as well as the earnest learning attitude of the participants, the course was successfully completed and left a strong positive impression.

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute, delivered a speech

In the speech at the closing ceremony, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute, expressed her sincere gratitude for the special attention and enthusiastic support from Professor Nguyen Van Tuan and Dr. Tran Son Thach.

"The two professors, regardless of difficulties, traveled from the distant land of Australia to Vietnam to engage in academic exchange with the future scientists of the country. Particularly, the course has been honored to have dedicated support and collaboration from the Board of Directors of Institute for Public Policy and Rural Development, who have graciously provided Phuong Nam Institute with state-of-the-art facilities and equipment to support the course."

According to Ms. Xuan Vy, scientific research plays a crucial role in achieving the strategic development goals of the nation. Notably, the course has been organized in alignment with the current policies of the Party and State at enhancing to: “Increase the number of inventions, patents, and scientific works in regional and global ranking list; enhance the production of scientific and technological products, as well as outstanding literary and artistic works; and publication of research in prestigious domestic and international journals; improve the quality of Vietnam’s scientific journals to meet regional and international standards.

With the positive feedback and evaluations from the participants, Phuong Nam Institute pledges to organize a greater number of  constructive courses in the future, particularly, the courses on the application of artificial intelligence (AI) and data analysis using R tools in scientific research.

Participants attending the closing ceremony of the expert course "Research Methodology and Scientific Publication."

At the conclusion of the ceremony, 168 participants were honored with certificates for their outstanding achievement. This is not only a recognition of their efforts throughout the learning process but also a testament to their effective acquisition of knowledge and skills in research methodology and scientific publication.

After completing the course, Phuong Nam Institute expect each participant to become a pioneering core member, playing a key role in enhancing the scientific standing, promoting sustainable development, and expanding Vietnam's influence in the region and globally.

Guided by the vision of “Providing society with developed resources enhanced to international standards” and the mission of “Serving by Trusted Values”, Phuong Nam Institute is fully aware of our responsibility to ensure authentic, positive, and dedicated values in all of its activities, as demonstrated by the consistent organization of similar training courses.

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE AND THE UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCES HAVE SIGNED A MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING TO PROMOTE TRAINING ACTIVITIES AND ENHANCE PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCIES
tieudeseo: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE AND THE UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCES HAVE SIGNED A MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING TO PROMOTE TRAINING ACTIVITIES AND ENHANCE PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCIES
mieutangan: On January 11, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) and University of Sciences, Hue University, signed a Memorandum of Understanding, paving the way for numerous collaboration opportunities and remarkable growth.
mieutachitiet:
Attending the ceremony, on behalf of University of Sciences, Hue University, were Assoc. Prof. Dr. Vo Thanh Tung, Rector; Dr. Tran Thanh Luong, Head of the Undergraduate Academic and Student Affairs Department; and Dr. Hoang Dinh Trung, Head of the Postgraduate Training Department. On behalf of Phuong Nam Institute, there were Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Phuoc, Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Union of Science and Technology Associations, Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Scientific Council, Chairman of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board; Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director, along with distinguished members of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board.

 

The delegates and participants of the Journalism Training Course took a commemorative photo at the ceremony.

 

Promote training cooperation and enhance the quality of human resources

With the desire to contribute to the development of social resources in Ho Chi Minh City in particular and the southern region in general, Phuong Nam Institute and University of Sciences, Hue University, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding, creating new opportunities for training and enhancing enrollment capabilities, as well as promoting the development of scientific and technological activities. This event marks a strong commitment between the two institutions, with a shared goal of building a comprehensive education system that meets the growing demands of society and fosters international integration.

 

The leaders of the two institutions signed the Memorandum of Understanding.

 

The Memorandum of Understanding focuses on coordinating professional enrollment activities, diversifying training programs, and boosting the promotion and branding of both institutions; Collaborating on scientific research subjects that align with the needs and capabilities of both parties; Jointly organizing both long-term and short-term training courses to enhance reputation in the field of education and training.

 

The delegates participating in the signing ceremony took a commemorative photo together.

 

The provisions in the Memorandum of Understanding between the two institutions serve as a basis to reinforce their commitment to advancing social responsibility, improving education quality, and promoting scientific research both regionally and nationally. This agreement provides a solid foundation for both institutions to jointly achieve new milestones, contributing to the building of a developed, civilized, and sustainable society.

At the ceremony, Phuong Nam Institute, in collaboration with University of Sciences, Hue University, also held a solemn graduation ceremony and awarded Journalism Training Certificates to the 33 outstanding students of the 61st course who successfully completed the program. This marks an initial success in the cooperation between the two institutions and is expected to pave the way for many groundbreaking training programs that meet practical needs and the evolving trends of society.

 

Assoc. Prof. Dr. Vo Thanh Tung, Rector, delivered the closing speech for the course.

 

The Journalism Training Course consists of 8 modules: Skills in Broadcast journalism, skills in digital and multimedia journalism, news writing skills, article writing skills, interview and investigative journalism skills, video and photojournalism skills, press conference organization and press release writing, and journalism skills and editorial practices.

 

Assoc. Prof. Dr. Vo Thanh Tung and Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy jointly awarded certificates to the learners.

 

On behalf of the class, Mr. Le Trong Thanh expressed expressed gratitude to the faculty of University of Sciences, Hue University and Phuong Nam Institute for their continuous support during the course. He also emphasized that the learners are actively applying the knowledge they acquired to their journalistic work in various agencies and organizations, with the goal of meeting the needs of innovation, international integration, and contributing to the country's continued success in this era of development.

 

On behalf of the class, a bouquet of flowers was presented to express gratitude to the leaders of both institutions.

 

The 61st Journalism Training Course has marked a significant milestone in establishing long-lasting partnerships, contributing to the development of a high-quality journalism workforce and addressing the growing and diverse demands of communications.

Strategic foundation for sustainable development.

At this ceremony, the Phuong Nam Institute presented the Decisions to recognize new members of the Scientific Advisory Board to three distinguished experts:

  1. Do Van Muoi, MA, Honorary Consul of the Republic of Estonia in Ho Chi Minh City;
  2. Dr. Nguyen Tan Khuyen, University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City;
  3. Prof. Dr. Ho Duc Hung, University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City, Dean of The Institute of Education Sciences and Training Research (IES).

 

The new members of the Scientific Advisory Board took a commemorative photo with the leaders of the Institute's Scientific Advisory Board.

 

Through this event, the Phuong Nam Institute looks forward to continued support and collaboration from the esteemed members of the Scientific Advisory Board to further advance groundbreaking research projects. With the vision of "Providing society with developed resources enhanced to international standards" the Phuong Nam Institute is deeply committed to ensuring authentic, positive, and dedicated value in all research activities, constantly innovating and improving to become one of the leading research centers in the region, establishing our position on the international scientific map.

PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE AND THE UNIVERSITY OF SCIENCES HAVE SIGNED A MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING TO PROMOTE TRAINING ACTIVITIES AND ENHANCE PROFESSIONAL COMPETENCIES
On January 11, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) and the University of Sciences, Hue University, signed a Memorandom of Understanding, paving the way for numerous collaboration opportunities and remarkable growth.

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE HAS SIGNED MEMORANDA OF UNDERSTANDING ON COOPERATION WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF LAW AND INTERNATIONAL FRANCOPHONE INSTITUTE,  VIETNAM NATIONAL UNIVERSITY, HANOI
mieutangan: Aiming to develop high-quality human resources and establish professional training programs, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) has made significant progress in signing cooperation agreement to strengthen and expand relationships, improve the quality of education, and meet the social development needs.
mieutachitiet:
Aiming to develop high-quality human resources and establish professional training programs, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) has made significant progress in signing cooperation agreement to strengthen and expand relationships, improve the quality of education, and meet the social development needs.

Cooperation in sustainable development and the enhancement of educational quality

 On January 13, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute had a meeting and discussion with the leadership of the University of Law - Vietnam National University, Hanoi (VNU), regarding the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on cooperation. Attending the ceremony, on the side of the VNU University of Law, were Assoc. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Trong Diep, Chairman of the University Council; Assoc. Prof. Dr. Trinh Tien Viet, Vice Presiden, along with leaders from several departments of the university. On the side of Phuong Nam Institute, was Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director, and distinguished faculty members from the Institute's leadership.

Both leaders signed a Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation.

In his speech at the ceremony, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Trinh Tien Viet emphasized that the signing of the Memorandum of Understanding is the beginning of sustainable development between the VNU University of Law and Phuong Nam Institute. This event not only reaffirms the mutual commitment of both parties to promote education and research but also paves the way for the future opportunities for both organizations.

Assoc. Prof. Dr. Trinh Tien Viet gave the speech at the ceremony.

Regarding the cooperation program between the two organizations, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy emphasized that the Memorandum of Understanding serves as a foundation for Phuong Nam Institute and the VNU University of Law to build detailed cooperation plans, effectively utilize the resources and advantages of each party to achieve the set goals. Furthermore, this event facilitates opportunities for international collaboration, building a bridge to strengthen the long-term relationship between the two organizations.

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy made a statement at the ceremony.

At the Memorandom of Understanding signing ceremony, Phuong Nam Institute and the VNU University of Law launched collaborative activities, specifically focusing on: cooperation in consulting and enrolling for training programs; building, improving, developing and organizing training programs to cultivate high-quality legal workforce for the Southern region and nationwide; implementing short-term training programs, legal knowledge enrichment courses, professional training in the legal field, and capacity-building workshops on scientific management, innovation, and leadership for managers, specialists, and lecturers;...

The leaders of both organizations had discussions at the ceremony.

The leaders of both sides believe that the signing of the Memorandum of Understanding will create a solid foundation for enhanced cooperation in the fields of education and scientific research, contributing to the sustainable development of the national education system.

Cooperation the development of professional training programs.

In December 2024, Phuong Nam Institute and the leadership of the International Francophone Institute – Vietnam National University, Hanoi, also discussed and signed a Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation. Attending the ceremony, on behalf of the International Francophone Institute was Assoc. Prof. Dr. Phung Danh Thang, Director, along with leaders from the faculty's departments. On behalf of Phuong Nam Institute, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director, along with distinguished faculty members of the Institute's leadership, attended the ceremony.

The leaders and faculty members took a commemorative photo together at the ceremony.

To maximize the effectiveness of the functions and responsibilities, Phuong Nam Institute and the International Francophone Institute – Vietnam National University, Ha Noi, have engaged in discussions and reached an agreement to collaborate and grow together across multiple fields, with a primary focus on training and research, drawing on the unique strengths of each party.

Specifically, the Memorandum of Understanding determines the main directions of cooperation, including: collaboration in the field of education; collaboration in the field of scientific research; and collaboration in the fields of information and communication.

Both leaders signed a Memorandom of Understanding on cooperation.

The signing of the two Memoranda of Understanding reflects Phuong Nam Institute's proactive spirit and commitment to promoting cooperation, training, research, and the development of high-quality human resources. Thanks to the clear and consistent terms of the agreement, the Institute is expected to contribute to creating trusted value serving society and the community.

With the mission: "Serving by Trusted Values" Phuong Nam Institute understands our responsibility in ensuring authentic values, promoting research collaboration, training, and the development of high-quality human resources. The signing of the Memoranda of Understanding marks a significant milestone, contributing to the expansion of the Institute's strategic partner network and enhancing our position in education and research in Vietnam and across the region.

tieude: ADVANCING VIETNAMESE SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH IN THE CONTEXT OF INTERNATIONAL INTEGRATION
tieudeseo: ADVANCING VIETNAMESE SCIENTIFIC RESEARCH IN THE CONTEXT OF INTERNATIONAL INTEGRATION
mieutangan: Workshop “Research Methodology and Scientific Publication” attracted substantial attention from numerous researchers, offering new perspectives into the potential and strategic directions for scientific research in Vietnam.
mieutachitiet:
On December 22, 2024, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) successfully collaborated with Institute for Public Policy & Rural Development to organize workshop “Research Methodology and Scientific Publication”. The program drew nearly 200 researchers working at educational institutions, healthcare facilities, and businesses across the country.

The overview of workshop “Research Methodology and Scientific Publication”

The presiding panel of workshop consists of scientists with extensive experience in scientific research and international publication, including:

1. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Phuoc, Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Union of Science and Technology Associations, Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City Scientific Council, Chairman of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board;

2. Dr. Nguyen Trung Dong, Director of Institute for Public Policy & Rural Development, Member of Phuong Nam Institute’s Scientific Advisory Board;

3. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan, Director of the Centre for Health Technologies at the University of Technology Sydney, Australia;

4  Dr. Tran Son Thach, Lecturer at the University of Technology Sydney, Australia.

The presiding panel of workshop “Research Methodology and Scientific Publication” (From left to right: Dr. Nguyen Trung Dong, Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Phuoc, Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan and Dr. Tran Son Thach)

The workshop offered an overview and essential knowledge about the scientific research process, while helping researchers clearly identify the opportunities and challenges in establishing Vietnam's scientific position on the international stage.      

Challenges from quality to research ethics

At this event, Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan stated that the number of scientific papers from Vietnam surpassed 20,000, double the figure from 10 years ago. This is an encouraging sign. It demonstrates that agencies, organizations, and the government are making increasing investments in scientific research and development. However, Professor Tuan also warned about the issue of quality, which is not keeping up with the growth in quantity. Professor Tuan emphasized that there are several current ethical issues in scientific research. If not addressed promptly, these issues could become significant challenges that will impact the quality of research in the future.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan discussed the issue of ethics in scientific research at the workshop

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan addressed the ethical issues in research, which are emerging as a major challenge. “In just the past few weeks, 4 to 5 research papers in Vietnam have been withdrawn due to authorship disputes. This is a serious issue that could have a significant impact on the reputation of Vietnamese science in the future if it is not controlled”, Professor Tuan said.

In addition, Professor Tuan also pointed out the current issues of data fabrication, plagiarism, and violations of research rules... These issues not only harm the reputation of individuals but also seriously affect the trust in Vietnamese science as a whole.

According to Dr. Nguyen Trung Dong, one of the major challenges in scientific research is digital transformation, the application of science, and promotion of effective development cooperation.

On the other hand, during the research process, researchers need to clearly identify practical needs and then conduct scientific projects to address these issues.

At the same time, there should be support from a team of experienced experts to enhance the capabilities of researchers, ensure the quality and effectiveness of scientific works.

Dr. Nguyen Trung Dong delivered a speech at the workshop

Sustainable development directions for Vietnamese science

In the context of science and technology becoming a key driver for sustainable development and global competition among nations, Vietnam is also part of this trend. Grasping and applying scientific and technological achievements is not only an urgent requirement but also an opportunity to enhance the nation's position, promote innovation, and build a modern knowledge-based economy.

At the workshop, Prof. Nguyen Van Phuoc shared his concerns: “In the new era, science, technology, and human capital will be the foundation determining the sustainable development of the country.” Professor Phuoc emphasized that to achieve sustainable development goals, we must continuously invest in the quality of research, develop human potential, and build a transparent and professional scientific management environment.

Professor Nguyen Van Phuoc shared his concerns regarding the current quality of scientific research

Professor Nguyen Van Phuoc also shared insights on the scientific research development program in Ho Chi Minh City, “It is planned to invest in the construction of innovation centers and research and development centers that meet international standards. The goal is for Ho Chi Minh City to have at least five internationally recognized centers by 2030. It has been and continues to open up many opportunities for leading, high-quality scientists to conduct research”.

Looking towards the future, Vietnam is striving to become the leading scientific research hub in the southern region. However, to turn this goal into reality, we need to focus on improving the quality of international publications, ensuring compliance with research ethics, and developing a long-term scientific development strategy, aiming for a sustainable scientific community.

Building a solid scientific foundation to become a key driver of national development

The workshop not only provided knowledge but also served as a platform for researchers to share experiences and contribute their ideas. With proper investment and relentless efforts, Vietnamese science has the potential to thrive and become a core driving force for economic, social, and technological development in the new era. “Recently, there have been more flexible policies in scientific research, an increase in research projects, and better investment in machinery and equipment. The national policy allocates 1% of GDP to scientific research”, Professor Phuoc said.

Dr. Bui Le Cuong - Deputy Head of Hematology Department, Cho Ray Hospital discussed at the workshop

Vietnam has a great opportunity to make a new leap in science and technology, but it also faces significant pressures from the global research market. Ensuring the quality of publications, enhancing research ethics, and creating a supportive environment for the development of science will be key to the long-term growth of Vietnamese science.

Science is both the foundation for enhancing the nation's standing and a crucial driver of socio-economic development. With the current efforts, Vietnam is fully capable of transforming into a leading scientific research hub in the region.

With the mission of “Providing society with quality resources”, Phuong Nam Institute focuses on “Researching scientific subjects, projects and transferring quality technology” to contribute to the sustainable development of social resources in the future. The institute continuously builds a modern training environment, where top-tier scientists, experts, and lecturers from both within country and abroad come together. With a long-term vision, Phuong Nam Institute is committed to developing advanced training programs, research, and technology transfer that align with the practical needs of society, while fostering innovation to enhance human resource quality and contribute to building a strong, connected, and highly potential academic and professional community.

tieude: Party chief receives prominent intellectuals, scientists nationwide
tieudeseo: Party chief receives prominent intellectuals, scientists nationwide
mieutangan: VTV.vn - Party General Secretary To Lam hosted a reception in Hanoi on December 30 for 200 prominent intellectuals and scientists who are currently working in various fields of research, application, education, and training nationwide.
mieutachitiet:
Recognising the contributions by intellectuals and scientists to the country’s achievements, especially during 40 years of renewal, the Party chief said that the Party, State and people expect their continued contributions as science, technology and innovation is the way that help the country catch up with the world and surpass the nation itself and the world.

He pointed to the need for the Party, State and all-level administration to make strong renovation in training, recruiting and using intellectuals and scientists.

The leader urged intellectuals and scientists to fulfil their responsibilities and missions in the new revolutionary phase, joining hands with the entire Party, military and people to achieve strategic goals, turning the country into a developed, high-income nation by 2045, on a par with global powers.

The effective and early implementation of the Politburo’s Resolution No. 57 on breakthroughs in the development of science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation will make an inspiration, new momentum, and a new frontier for creativity for them, he held.

The Party chief expressed his hope that intellectuals and scientists will play the key role in elevating Vietnam to the top three Southeast Asian countries in research and development of artificial intelligence, and among the top 50 nations globally in terms of digital competitiveness and e-government development. By 2030, Vietnam should have at least five digital technology companies on par with global tech powers. By 2045, Vietnam will be one of the global technology hubs, ranking among the top 30 countries in innovation and digital transformation, he said.

He also called on intellectuals and scientists to exert more efforts in training the next generation of intellectuals, while reinforcing the workers-peasants-intellectuals alliance in the new context, attracting Vietnamese intellectuals working abroad and international intellectuals to contribute to the country's development, and strengthening cooperation with overseas Vietnamese intellectuals and foreign scientists in transferring, developing science, technology, innovation, and digital transformation is essential, especially in new fields such as digital and green transformation.

Particularly, it is crucial to develop a high-quality intellectual contingent and human resources, with the main role played by educators who should primarily be scientists and intellectuals, with plans to train leading scientists in fields crucial for the country's development, such as artificial intelligence, digital and green transformation, quantum computing, and biosciences, he stressed.

The Party General Secretary also pointed to the need for a closer link among scientific centres, research institutes, schools, and enterprises, and the necessity for intellectual property laws and regulations to be perfected.

To ensure the country confidently steps into a new era, the era of the nation’s rise, he emphasised that the Party, State, and people place great trust and expectation in intellectuals and scientists, who are the pioneers driving innovation and breakthroughs, accelerating the goal of fast and sustainable national development in the new period.

tieude: HCMC mobilizes resources to create breakthroughs in science, technology
tieudeseo: HCMC mobilizes resources to create breakthroughs in science, technology
mieutangan: Ho Chi Minh City will focus on reviewing and investing in infrastructure, mobilizing all financial, human, and other resources to achieve breakthroughs in science and technology innovation, and digital transformation in the coming time.
mieutachitiet:
Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee Phan Van Mai (Photo: SGGP)

Chairman of the Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee Phan Van Mai said in an interview with Sai Gon Giai Phong Newspaper on the implementation of Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW of the Politburo on breakthroughs in science and technology development, innovation, and national digital transformation.

The development of science and technology, innovation, and digital transformation are key breakthroughs for Ho Chi Minh City's growth in the new era. Therefore, the city is closely aligned with the targets and tasks outlined in Resolution No. 57.

Among these goals, the R&D (Research and Development) Fund is expected to reach 2 percent of the GRDP by 2030, and the investment in scientific research, technology development, innovation, and digital transformation will account for 3 percent of the annual state budget, he said.

The city will strive to become one of the country’s leading digital technology industrial hubs and the top localities with science, technology, and innovation potential. The city also sets targets to rank among the top five provinces and cities with the Provincial Competitiveness Index and the top three localities with e-government and digital government indexes. Additionally, the digital economy is expected to contribute around 40 percent of the GRDP.

To mobilize all resources for breakthrough development in science and technology, innovation, and digital transformation in the coming time, Ho Chi Minh City will continue to research and update new policies, such as corporate income tax (CIT) exemption for innovative startup enterprises, personal income tax exemption for high-tech personnel, and policies to attract talents in the science and technology sector.

In the coming time, the city will consider issuing additional support policies, such as those offering financial assistance to science and technology businesses to improve product quality and develop new products, providing shared applications and workforce training programs, and co-investing or offering incentive loans to support businesses investing in R&D (research and development) laboratories, operations, research, or training activities.

Additionally, the city will offer tuition assistance and support for advanced training in digital transformation, science and technology, international training costs, attending international conferences, or participating in international research projects.

For digital transformation, the city focuses on all three pillars, including digital government, digital economy, and digital society, and strives to reach at least 80 percent of administrative procedures and documents to be addressed online by the end of 2025.

Regarding digital society, the city will complete the infrastructure for information technology and telecommunications and introduce many applications for citizens and businesses, particularly in the sectors of healthcare, education, employment, and social welfare. The southern metropolis will also pay attention to the digital economy's development, especially supporting the city’s businesses in implementing digital transformation.

Ho Chi Minh City currently has around 2,200 innovative startup enterprises, accounting for 50 percent of the total number of the country, including more than 200 venture capitals, 53 startup support organizations, and 97 universities and colleges. Over 500 startup events and nearly 80 startup competitions are organized every year in the city. In 2024, Ho Chi Minh City ranked 111th in the global ranking of cities with the most dynamic innovation startup ecosystems.

The city is urgently working with Da Nang City and the Ministry of Planning and Investment to prepare a dossier on promulgating mechanisms and policies for the financial center in the city and submit it to the National Assembly for approval. These policies are created to attract investors and various stakeholders to participate in the financial center.

Ho Chi Minh City is also planning investments in infrastructure, including transportation, energy connectivity, information technology, and other essential facilities.

By Ngo Binh – Translated by Kim Khanh

tieude: Central steering committee for science-technology development, innovation set up
mieutangan: A decision on the formation of the Central Steering Committee for the Development of Science, Technology, Innovation and Digital Transformation was announced.
mieutachitiet:
Participants at the national conference held by the Politburo and the Party Central Committee’s Secretariat in Hanoi on January 13 morning. (Photo: SGGP)

The statement was made at a national conference held by the Politburo and the Party Central Committee’s Secretariat in Hanoi on January 13 morning.

According to the Politburo’s Decision No. 229 dated January 10, 2025, the Steering Committee, headed by Party General Secretary To Lam, consists of 19 members. It is responsible for leading and directing the implementation of breakthrough solutions to further promote science – technology development and innovation, and speed up national digital transformation.

The Steering Committee is tasked with researching and proposing the Politburo and the Party Central Committee’s Secretariat consider and make decisions on mechanisms, legal policies, and breakthrough solutions for accelerating science-technology development, innovation, and digital transformation.

It will also lead and coordinate with party committees at all levels to guide, inspect, and supervise the implementation of relevant resolutions and regulations of the Party, and the State's laws; and review relevant legal mechanisms, policies, and administrative procedures, thus making recommendations for authorities to consider and deploy.

It will also work on directing party committees and organisations at all levels to inspect and take strict action against party organisations and members who delay, obstruct, or fail to implement the solutions and tasks assigned by authorised agencies in this work, or violate during the implementation process.

The Steering Committee has the authority to set up a support team and a national advisory council for science - technology, innovation, and digital transformation.

Vietnamplus

tieude: Party chief chairs 1st meeting of steering committee for science, technology development
tieudeseo: Party chief chairs 1st meeting of steering committee for science, technology development
mieutangan: Party General Secretary To Lam chaired the first meeting of the Central Steering Committee for the Development of Science, Technology, Innovation and Digital Transformation in Hanoi on January 20.
mieutachitiet:
Party General Secretary To Lam (Photo: VNA)

Addressing the event, Lam, who is head of the steering committee, stressed that the implementation of tasks should focus on and prioritise the core issues outlined in the Politburo's Resolution No.57-NQ/TW, dated on December 22, 2024, on breakthroughs in science and technology development, innovation, and national digital transformation, with emphasis placed on removing institutional barriers to the development of science and technology.

The steering committee must ensure effective operations, avoid formalities and not perform the responsibilities of functional agencies, he said, adding that it should promote the role of each member and listen to ideas and opinions from leading experts and feedback from the grassroots level to identify urgent, fundamental, and breakthrough issues to focus on.

Tasks should be carried out with determination and urgency; and each must be specifically assigned, with measurable indicators and clear timelines, Lam noted.

Regarding a national advisory council, the General Secretary emphasised that the council’s structure should ensure a balanced representation of leading scientists and experts in various fields, with priority given to those who are reputable and have outstanding achievements. The council should also include representatives from businesses and managers to ensure practical effectiveness.

He appreciated the proposal to develop an index for monitoring, supervising, and evaluating the implementation of Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW, and requested that it be urgently completed and put into use in the second quarter of 2025 to accurately assess results and promptly address bottlenecks.

He asked the Prime Minister, who is a deputy head of the steering committee, to direct relevant agencies to launch an emulation movement across the political system to implement the resolution; research and propose reforms to the financial management mechanism for carrying out science, technology, innovation, and digital transformation tasks; simplify administrative procedures as much as possible; and grant autonomy in the use of funding for science and technology research and development.

The Government should also have a specific plan to develop a high-quality workforce, especially scientific and technological talent, and create a draft special mechanism to attract highly skilled Vietnamese expatriates and foreigners to work and live in Vietnam, thus contributing to the country’s science and technology development, said Lam.

For the steering committee’s members, the Party leader asked the Minister of Public Security to coordinate the development of an ecosystem for Project 06 on developing applications of residents data and electronic identification and authentication to serve digital transformation, and the National Data Centre to put these services into use soon.

The Minister of Information and Communications was tasked with overseeing the nationwide rollout of the 5G broadband mobile network, while the Minister of Science and Technology was required to lead the development and submission of a strategic technology and industry development programme for approval by competent authorities.

Lam also requested the Minister of Finance to allocate state budget funding for the scientific mission to support strategic technologies, in line with the requirements of Resolution No. 57, and the Minister of Planning and Investment to order drafting and submitting a public-private partnership policy for the development of science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation to competent authorities for approval.
