mieutachitiet:
PROFESSOR NGUYEN VAN TUAN DIRECTLY CONDUCTS THE TRAINING COURSE TITLED

"MASTERING EVIDENCE-BASED MEDICINE: CRITICAL APPRAISAL AND APPLICATION"

Pursuant to the consensus of the People’s Committee of Gia Lai Province and with the aim of contributing to the implementation of Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW, Binh Dinh General Hospital, in collaboration with the Phuong Nam Institute, respectfully informs about the organization of the training course titled “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application”, conducted by Professor Nguyen Van Tuan, one of the world’s top 1% experts in the medical field.

* FACILITATORS:

1. Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan (Principal Lecturer)

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan is a Distinguished Professor at the University of Technology Sydney (Australia), Director of the Centre for Health Technologies at the University of Technology Sydney and the first Vietnamese to become a member of the Australian Academy of Health and Medical Sciences. Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan is one of the few Vietnamese experts abroad ranked among the top 1% of global experts, with over 300 WoS (ISI) publications and numerous articles published in prestigious international journals such as Nature, Science, Lancet, New England Journal of Medicine, JAMA, etc.

Homepage of Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan at UTS: link

2. Dr. Tran Son Thach (Teaching Assistant)

Dr. Tran Son Thach is a lecturer in Biomedical Engineering at the University of Technology Sydney (Australia), with over 15 years of experience in research design and data analysis. His expertise includes complex data modeling from multi-center randomized controlled trials, predictive model development, linked health data analysis, and the management of large datasets. He has published 68 peer-reviewed original publications and 3 systematic reviews in the top peer-reviewed journals such as The N Engl J Med, the Lancet, Lancet Child & Adolescent Health, JAMA, JAMA Netw Open, PLoS Med, Eur Respir J, J Bone Miner Res, J Clin Endocrinol Metab, Clin Chem, and Diabetes Care. Within the last 5 years, he published 45 original papers and 1 Cochrane review.

* TIME & VENUE

1. Time:

Commencement date: 7:30 AM, August 26, 2025 (Tuesday)

Course length: 03 days (from Aug 26, 2025 to Aug 28, 2025)

Learning format: Offline or Online

Class schedule:

  • Morning: 8:00 AM - 11:30 AM
  • Afternoon: 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM

2. Venue: Binh Dinh General Hospital - 39A Pham Ngoc Thach Street, Quy Nhon Ward, Gia Lai Province, Vietnam

* COURSE CONTENT

The course content consists of 10 lectures

August 26, 2025:

Lecture 1: Introduction to Evidence-Based Medicine

Lecture 2: Literature Searching

Lecture 3: Clinical Research Designs

Lecture 4: Appraising Research: Bias and Confounding

August 27, 2025:

Lecture 5: Interpreting Results: p-Values and 95% Confidence Intervals

Lecture 6: Interpreting Results: Absolute Differences

Lecture 7: Interpreting Results: Odds Ratios and Risk Ratios

August 28, 2025:

Lecture 8: Interpreting Diagnostic Study Results

Lecture 9: Appraising Research: QALY (Quality-Adjusted Life Years)

Lecture 10: Sample Size Estimation

The tuition fee includes name badge, material, certificate of completion and refreshments.

mieutangan: On August 18, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) solemnly held the opening ceremony for the expert course entitled “Research Design and Scientific Publication” at 45 Dinh Tien Hoang Street, Saigon Ward, Ho Chi Minh City. The course attracted more than 120 participants from 70 educational institutions, healthcare organizations, and enterprises in 13 provinces and cities nationwide.
mieutachitiet:
Overview of the opening ceremony of the "Research Design and Scientific Publication" course.

The ceremony was honored by the presence of:

1. Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of the Phuong Nam Institute;

2. Professor Nguyen Van Tuan – Director of the Centre for Health Technologies, University of Technology Sydney (Australia);

3. Dr. Tran Son Thach – University of Technology Sydney (Australia);

In her opening remarks, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of the Phuong Nam Institute, emphasized the main objective of the program: It is designed to enhance the scientific research capacity of researchers and lecturers, covering the whole process from developing ideas to conducting research and writing scientific papers.

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy making a speech at the opening ceremony.

At the opening ceremony, Professor Nguyen Van Tuan highlighted the importance of strengthening academic standards: Over the past decade, we have been actively engaged in what could be called the transfer of methodologies for preparing scientific papers that meet international standards, with the hope of thereby enhancing the presence of science in the global arena.

Professor Nguyen Van Tuan delivering his speech at the opening ceremony.

The five-day course is led by Professor Nguyen Van Tuan – one of the top 1% most cited experts in the field of Medicine globally. The program consists of 16 in-depth lectures, focusing on two main areas: research design and scientific writing in accordance with international standards. The course aims to strengthen participants’ research capabilities  from idea conceptualization, research implementation, to scholarly article development. Additionally, it equips learners with the essential skills to prepare and publish scientific papers in international journals.

Participants attending the course's opening ceremony.

Aligned with Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW issued by the Politburo on breakthrough development in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation, Phuong Nam Institute organised the course standing as a key activity within its 2025 training agenda. The course emphasizes standardization of research processes, enhancement of scientific rigor, minimization of bias, and assurance of result reliability. A pivotal objective is to familiarize participants with scientific publication standards from manuscript preparation to peer-review response  ultimately increasing publication success in high-impact academic journals.

Professor Nguyen Van Tuan, Dr. Tran Son Thach, and Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy taking a commemorative photograph with participants.

The “Research Design and Scientific Publication” course is not only a high-impact professional development program, but also reflects Phuong Nam Institute’s commitment to bridging global knowledge with the practical research landscape in Vietnam. The course is expected to contribute significantly to the elevation of scientific standards, foster scientific publications and progressively develop a highly competent research community deeply integrated with the global scientific ecosystem.

Furthermore, the course equipped participants with essential skills for preparing and submitting manuscripts to international journals.

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE AND BINH DINH GENERAL HOSPITAL ORGANISED OPENING CEREMONY FOR THE TRAINING COURSE "MASTERING EVIDENCE-BASED MEDICINE: CRITICAL APPRAISAL AND APPLICATION"
mieutangan: On August 26, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute), in collaboration with Binh Dinh General Hospital, held the opening ceremony for the training course titled “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application”, led by Professor Nguyen Van Tuan - Professor ranked among the world’s top 0.5% of scientists, Distinguished Professor at University of Technology Sydney.
mieutachitiet:
An overview of the opening ceremony of the training course on “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application”.

The ceremonyy was honored by the presence of distinguished guests, including:

  1. Mr. Do Van Tu - Director of Binh Dinh General Hospital Joint Stock Company;
  2. Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy - Director of Phuong Nam Institute;
  3. Professor Nguyen Van Tuan - Director of the Centre for Health Technologies, University of Technology Sydney (Australia);
  4. Dr. Tran Son Thach - University of Technology Sydney (Australia).

In the opening speech for the training course, Mr. Do Van Tu - Director of Binh Dinh General Hospital Joint Stock Company expressed, "This is an opportunity for participants and distinguished guests to unite and connect, forming a supportive community in scientific research and critical appraisal of medical evidence. From this, we can produce more research projects to further strengthen medical development in Vietnam and publish medical works with international recognition."

Mr. Do Van Tu - Director of Binh Dinh General Hospital Joint Stock Company - delivering a speech at the ceremony.

At the opening ceremony, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy - Director of Phuong Nam Institute - gave a speech, "In order to contribute to the implementation of Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW issued by the Politburo on breakthrough development in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation; with the desire to help improve the quality of human resources in the field of science in general and medicine in particular - in line with international standards. With the approval of the People's Committee of Gia Lai Province under official letter No. 1687/UBND-KTTH, permitting Binh Dinh General Hospital to collaborate with Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development to organize the training course titled "Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application.""

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy - Director of Phuong Nam Institute - giving a speech at the opening ceremony of the training course.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan - Director of the Centre for Health Technologies, University of Technology Sydney (Australia) - shared at the ceremony, "Evidence-based medicine is actually not something new. It has appeared in the medical literature and in the medical world since the early 1990s. By now, it has been over 30 years. It is not new to some of our colleagues, but perhaps it is new to us here. Because we have limited access to medical literature and limited training programs in this field within the country, some skills and information have not been fully accessible to our colleagues. That is why we designed this program."

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan sharing about the training course “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application”

Specialist Level II Doctor Le Van Bon - a former student who attended Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan’s course 20 years ago and currently works at Binh Dinh Hospital, shared his thoughts, "We hope that this course, with the participation of leading professors, will provide us with more advanced knowledge in medical literature research, and that our future research outcomes will carry greater scientific value."

Specialist Level II Doctor Le Van Bon presenting flowers and taking commemorative photos with Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan and Dr. Tran Son Thach.

The opening ceremony of the training course on “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application” marks an important milestone in the effort to enhance the quality of training for local healthcare professionals and to promote the spirit of evidence-based medicine from central to grassroots levels.

The training course “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application” was led by Prof. Dr. Nguyen Van Tuan, with co-teaching and support from Dr. Tran Son Thach. The course consists of 10 lectures: Introduction to Evidence-Based Medicine; Literature Searching; Clinical Research Designs; Appraising Research: Bias and Confounding; Interpreting Results: p-Values and 95% Confidence Intervals; Interpreting Results: Absolute Differences; Interpreting Results: Odds Ratios and Risk Ratios; Interpreting Diagnostic Study Results; Appraising Research: QALY (Quality-Adjusted Life Years); Sample Size Estimation.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, and Mr. Do Van Tu taking commemorative photos with the training course participants.

Phuong Nam Institute expects that the training course “Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application” will contribute to the effective implementation of Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW issued by the Politburo on breakthrough development in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation. Through this training course, Phuong Nam Institute aims to promote the spirit of evidence-based medicine from central to grassroots levels, towards an efficient and science-based healthcare system; and to develop a high-quality healthcare workforce that meets the innovation demands of the sector in the digital transformation era.

Mr. Do Van Tu - Director of Binh Dinh General Hospital Joint Stock Company expressed, “This training course has provided valuable new knowledge and updates on global scientific research. I firmly believe that in the near future we will be able to apply these insights to advance our professional development and produce impactful, widely cited studies.”

Mr. Do Van Tu sharing at the closing ceremony.

In her address, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute - expressed deep appreciation for the dedication and support of Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan and Dr. Tran Son Thach, who had traveled from Australia to Vietnam to contribute to academic exchange. In addition, she expressed her gratitude for the support and collaboration of the Board of Directors as well as the relevant departments of Binh Dinh General Hospital.

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy making a speech at the closing ceremony.

At the conclusion of the ceremony, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute, announced the decision to award certificates of completion to the participants. This served as recognition of their dedicated learning, progressive spirit throughout the training course.

Mr. Do Van Tuan, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, and Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan taking commemorative photos with the participants.

The training course, titled "Mastering Evidence-Based Medicine: Critical Appraisal and Application", has helped strengthen the professional capacity of healthcare staff and progressively standardize healthcare practices towards a more scientific, effective, and sustainable approach. At the same time, it represents a concrete effort to realize the spirit of Resolution 57-NQ/TW on advancing breakthroughs in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation.

The training course is part of ongoing efforts to enhance research capacity, promote evidence-based medicine, and develop a high-quality healthcare workforce to meet the demands of digital transformation and international integration in the new era. Phuong Nam Institute aims to further expand specialized training programs on research design, critical appraisal, scientific publication, and medical data analysis in various localities in the near future.

tieude: TEACHER REFORMS, DIGITAL EXAMS TO RESHAPE VIETNAMESE EDUCATION: MINISTER
mieutangan: The Education Minister outlines a pivotal year focused on implementing curriculum reviews, new teacher policies with salary hikes, and piloting computerized exams to modernize the nation’s education system.
mieutachitiet:
First, Minister of Education and Training Nguyen Kim Son explained how the 2018 General Education Program should be implemented in the new school year of 2025-2026.

The Ministry of Education and Training (MoET) will conduct a comprehensive review of the program to assess its success in developing student qualities and competencies. This review will identify strengths and weaknesses to propose corrective measures, ensuring the program’s long-term effectiveness with objective, student-focused adjustments.

As to the ministry’s stance on managing extra classes, the MoET maintains its long-held position that while extra classes may offer some knowledge consolidation, they contribute little to a person’s overall development. The profound, negative consequences of rampant private tutoring demand continued and decisive intervention.

Therefore, in the 2025-2026 school year, the MoET will continue to direct and urge localities to issue formal regulations for managing extra classes. This aims to strengthen governmental accountability while requiring educational institutions to focus on the effective implementation of the official General Education Program.

Regarding the organization of all-day schooling (two sessions per day), the MoET has instructed schools to develop educational plans that clearly articulate the mobilization and use of resources for implementation where conditions permit. These plans must detail the content, duration, and target student groups, while ensuring teacher assignments are reasonable and compliant with regulations.

A key focus is on differentiated instruction, nurturing gifted students, providing revision for final-year students, and supporting those who are underperforming. Funding for the second session is primarily secured by the state budget, per the Prime Minister’s directive, with any social contributions adhering to current regulations.

Moving forward, the MoET will coordinate with the Ministry of Finance and local authorities to establish specific policies for financing this model.

Educators and staff in Tam Thanh Commune of Thanh Hoa Province are collaborating on cleaning their school campus in preparation for the new academic year’s inauguration ceremony

Moving on to the matter of specific time to pilot computerized national high school graduation exams, Minister Nguyen Kim Son informed that in preparation for this pilot from 2027 as directed by the Prime Minister, the MoET is concentrating on several key initiatives.

It is developing a comprehensive proposal for the exam’s organization, which will be submitted for Prime Ministerial approval in 2026. It’s also mobilizing experts to build a standardized question bank, slated for use in 2027, and formulating the necessary procedures and regulations for computer-based testing.

Furthermore, nationwide training and workshops are being organized, and it’s continuing its collaboration with the Government Cipher Committee on the secure transmission of exam materials and other critical security aspects.

Currently, the ministry is preparing the examination software system and is set to begin trials in various localities. It is anticipated that a pilot program involving over 100,000 students will be rolled out within this academic year.

Turning to the changes in teacher recruitment and policies applied in this academic year, the Minister shared that following the National Assembly’s passage of the Law on Teachers, the MoET has been developing a comprehensive set of implementation guidelines.

Specifically for recruitment, it is drafting a new circular that will likely assign provincial Departments of Education and Training as the lead agencies, or advise provincial authorities on appropriate decentralization.

This approach aims to reduce administrative intermediaries and synchronize recruitment quality, allowing a single application to be considered by multiple schools. It’s designed to save costs, expand opportunities for candidates, and help rectify localized teacher shortages or surpluses, ensuring a balanced staffing structure across all educational levels and subjects.

The MoET is also drafting a decree to detail specific articles of the Law on Teachers, including the content and format of recruitment. The plan is for a two-round process, consistent with current civil servant recruitment.

However, the second round, focusing on professional expertise, will be distinctively redesigned to mirror the practical realities of teaching. It will ensure a robust assessment of a candidate’s pedagogical skills for their specific grade level. This is expected to be a significant innovation, addressing the previous limitations of applying a generic civil service framework to the unique demands of the teaching profession.

The ministry is also finalizing draft regulations on salaries, allowances, and other policies to attract and support educators. Under this proposal, the base salary for all teachers is projected to increase by a minimum of approximately VND2 million (US$80) and potentially as much as VND5-7 million ($200-$280) per person per month. This increase applies only to the base salary and does not include other allowances.

Finally, Minister Nguyen Kim Son sent his message to teachers and students for this new school year. The 2025-2026 academic year is critically important, presenting the education sector with major tasks to accomplish and significant opportunities for innovation.

The keyword for this year is “implementation”. The focus is on rigorously implementing the educational policies of the Party and State, along with the 10 key tasks and solutions set by the sector itself. This is how it will overcome existing challenges and forge new paths for Vietnamese education.

Education is a centennial endeavor; it demands vision, persistence, fairness, and the utmost responsibility. He, therefore, calls upon everyone in the sector, from administrators to teachers and staff, along with all students, to prepare with the best possible mindset and conditions. Let Vietnam unite and join forces to make this new school year a busy, but joyful and successful one.

By Phan Thao – Translated by Thanh Tam

tieude: HCMC PUSHES FOR GREATER SCHOOL AUTONOMY AND DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION
tieudeseo: HCMC PUSHES FOR GREATER SCHOOL AUTONOMY AND DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION
mieutangan: Ho Chi Minh City's education sector is moving toward a future of greater autonomy for its public schools, a key focus for the upcoming 2025-2026 school year.
mieutachitiet:
The initiative was a major point of discussion at a recent conference held by the Ho Chi Minh City Department of Education and Training.

The meeting, which summarized achievements from the previous school year, revealed that the department now oversees a network of 251 public schools.

Looking ahead, the city plans to continue its push for educational excellence through several key projects. This includes a continued focus on international student assessment standards, as well as breakthrough programs to bring international certifications in English and Information Technology to students. These efforts are part of a broader, long-term plan to eventually establish English as the city's second language in schools.

Additionally, the department is prioritizing a comprehensive digital transformation. The entire city has already adopted a geographic information system (GIS) to streamline primary school enrollment, and similar digital initiatives are expected to be deployed across the sector.

Despite notable achievements, the education sector continues to encounter significant challenges. By the conclusion of the 2024–2025 academic year, the planning and management of the school network, infrastructure, and teaching equipment have not adequately met the demands of educational development.

Class sizes remain above the regulatory threshold, and the proportion of students attending full-day sessions has yet to reach the established targets. Investment in modern teaching equipment has been neither timely nor synchronized, hindering the effectiveness of instructional delivery.

Moreover, the workforce of educational managers, teachers, and staff remains insufficient in quantity and lacks structural uniformity. A portion of personnel has not met the required standards in foreign language proficiency and digital literacy, thereby limiting their capacity to adapt to educational innovation and international integration.

At the annual school year review conference, the Ho Chi Minh City Department of Education and Training issued a heartfelt appeal to the entire sector to support communities affected by the flooding caused by Storms No. 4 and No. 5.

Every contribution at this time, regardless of its size, holds immense value. It represents not only material assistance but also compassion and solidarity—a powerful source of encouragement to help affected individuals, especially students in flooded areas, return to school and continue their educational journey, stated Director Nguyen Van Hieu of the Ho Chi Minh City Department of Education and Training.

Looking ahead to the 2025–2026 academic year, Ho Chi Minh City will commission 1,434 newly constructed classrooms, funded by over VND4,500 billion from the state budget. Additionally, 180 classrooms financed through social investment will be brought into operation, ensuring that all children in the area have access to school placements.

The 15 key tasks for the 2025–2026 academic year:

  1. Improve institutional frameworks and enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of state management; reform school governance and promote autonomy for public educational institutions.
  2. Ensure equitable access to quality education for all learners; prioritize support for vulnerable groups including ethnic minorities, students in island communes, orphans, homeless children, persons with disabilities, and those from poor or near-poor households.
  3. Continue innovating and improving the quality of early childhood education, general education, and continuing education.
  4. Enhance, standardize, and develop the teaching workforce and educational management staff at all levels.
  5. Reform political and ideological education, as well as national defense and security education for teachers, students, and learners.
  6. Continue building the 'Happy School' model, fostering well-being and positive learning environments.
  7. Strengthen physical education, sports activities, and school health services; ensure school safety and proactively respond to natural disasters and epidemics.
  8. Improve the quality of workforce training and modernize vocational education.
  9. Promote international integration in education and training.
  10. Accelerate comprehensive digital transformation, applying digital technologies and artificial intelligence in education and training; continue administrative reform across the sector.
  11. Increase investment in educational infrastructure to support early childhood and general education programs; attract and effectively utilize resources to ensure successful implementation of the 2018 General Education Curriculum.
  12. Develop non-public education, expanding access and diversity in educational offerings.
  13. Strengthen and improve the effectiveness of inspection and legal enforcement in the education and training sector.
  14. Continue implementing emulation movements across the entire education sector.
  15. Effectively carry out educational communication efforts, raising awareness and engagement among stakeholders.

By Thu Tam - Translated by Anh Quan

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE ORGANIZED THE OPENING CEREMONY FOR THE TRAINING COURSE “APPLICATION OF AI IN NURSING PRACTICE”
tieudeseo: Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) held the opening ceremony for the training course “Application of AI in nursing practice”
mieutangan: On November 14, 2025, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) held the opening ceremony for the training course “Application of AI in nursing practice” at 45 Dinh Tien Hoang, Saigon Ward, Ho Chi Minh City. The course attracted more than 120 participants from 46 educational, healthcare, and business organizations, etc. across  provinces and cities nationwide.
mieutachitiet:
Overview of the opening ceremony of the training course “Application of AI in Nursing Practice”.

The ceremony was honored by the presence of:

  1. Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute;
  2. The instructor is Mr. Doan Hoang Duy, an expert in Artificial Intelligence (AI) from Duy Tan University.

In her opening remarks, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute stated, “Phuong Nam Institute is honored to organize the training course “Application of AI in Nursing Practice”, aiming to empower healthcare professionals with the ability to apply AI, enhance the quality of patient care, and optimize workflow efficiency in healthcare facilities.”

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy making a speech at the opening ceremony.

“I would like to express my sincere gratitude to Phuong Nam Institute for organizing a very meaningful and timely training course, which is also in line with the trend set out in Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW dated December 22, 2024 on breakthroughs in the development of science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation, as well as Resolution No. 72-NQ/TW dated September 9, 2025 on several breakthrough solutions to strengthen the protection, care, and improvement of public health.” shared Mr. Doan Hoang Duy at the opening ceremony.

Mr. Doan Hoang Duy delivering his speech at the opening ceremony.

The training course, conducted by Mr. Doan Hoang Duy, takes place over two days (November 14 - 15, 2025). The program is designed around four key modules: (1) Fundamentals of AI in Nursing Practice; (2) AI Applications in Patient Care; (3) AI Applications in Administration; (4) AI Applications in Management. The combination of 30% theory and 70% practice enables participants to directly experience AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and medical data analysis platforms in real-world work scenarios.

Participants attending the training course's opening ceremony.

To implement Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW issued by the Politburo on breakthrough development in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation, Phuong Nam Institute organized the training course “Application of AI in Nursing Practice” as part of our 2025 professional development program.

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy and Mr. Doan Hoang Duy taking commemorative photographs with participants.

The training course "Application of AI in Nursing Practice" not only provides highly practical professional development, but also reflects Phuong Nam Institute’s commitment to strengthening AI competencies among nursing staff, thereby enhancing patient care quality and optimizing healthcare operations.

Overview of the training course “Dissemination of Labor, Employment, and Social Insurance Laws.”

tieude: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE ORGANISED THE OPENING CEREMONY FOR THE EXPERT COURSE TITLED: "APPLICATIONS OF AI IN DATA ANALYSIS"
tieudeseo: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE ORGANISED THE OPENING CEREMONY FOR THE EXPERT COURSE TITLED: "APPLICATIONS OF AI IN DATA ANALYSIS"
mieutangan: On January 6, 2026, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (abbreviated as Phuong Nam Institute) held the opening ceremony for the expert course titled “Applications for AI in Data Analysis” in Ho Chi Minh City. The course attracted more than 130 participants from 92 educational institutions, healthcare organizations, research institutes, and enterprises across 19 provinces and cities in Vietnam, as well as from countries including the United States, Japan, and Denmark.
mieutachitiet:
Overview of the opening ceremony of the expert course “Applications for AI in Data Analysis”.

The ceremony was honored by the presence of:

1. Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute;

2. Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan, University of Technology Sydney (Australia);

In her opening remarks, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy, Director of Phuong Nam Institute, emphasized, “This course addresses how research can be conducted faster, deeper, and more effectively by transitioning from traditional approaches to a data-driven research model through AI. R excels in data storytelling: AI uncovers insights, while R enables their visualization.”

Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy making a speech at the opening ceremony.

“In the era of rapid AI development, scientists must cultivate three core skills: effective use of AI tools, prompt engineering to ensure high-quality outputs, and creative thinking - an irreplaceable human capacity. Overreliance on AI risks undermining independent thinking and the appreciation of genuine intellectual contributions.” Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan shared.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan sharing his insights at the opening ceremony.

Dr. Dinh Nguyen Thien Kim, MD, Specialist Level II, Director of Tan Hung General Hospital, is a distinguished learner who has continuously participated in four courses organized by Phuong Nam Institute since 2024, as follows:

  • The expert course “Research Methodology and Scientific Publication”, organized in December 2024.
  • The expert course “Data Analysis using R”, organized in May 2025.
  • The training course “Application of Evidence-Based Medicine”, organized in May 2025.
  • The expert course “Research Design and Scientific Publication”, organized in August 2025.

Today, Dr. Dinh continues to engage with Phuong Nam Institute through the expert course “Applications of AI in Data Analysis”.

Dr. Dinh Nguyen Thien Kim (MD, Specialist Level II) presenting flowers to congratulate Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan.

The six-day course (January 6 - 12, 2026; break on January 9) was led by Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan, who ranks among the top 0.05% of scholars worldwide in the fields of osteoporosis, bone density, and bone mineral (ScholarGPS, 2025), with online co-teaching via Zoom by Dr. Tran Son Thach and teaching assistance by Dr. Nguyen Gia Huy, both from the Garvan Institute of Medical Research, Australia.  The course program was designed with 21 in-depth lectures on key statistical methods for scientific data analysis. Participants will practice techniques for comparing differences between two groups of variables; Correlation analysis; Building and testing regression models; in particular, methods such as Meta-analysis, Propensity Score, Mendelian Randomization, and the applications of AI in data analysis.

Participants attending the expert course.

Aligned with Resolution No. 57-NQ/TW issued by the Politburo on breakthrough development in science, technology, innovation, and national digital transformation, Phuong Nam Institute organised this course standing as a key activity within our 2026 training agenda. The course aimed to standardize analytical procedures, improve research quality, and promote the application of modern technologies in data analysis.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan and Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy taking commemorative photographs with participants.

The expert course “Applications of AI in Data Analysis” was not only a highly practice-oriented professional development activity but also reflected Phuong Nam Institute’s efforts to connect international knowledge with research practice in Vietnam. The course aims to strengthen data analysis capacity, promote data-driven research, expand research collaboration, and advance the application of AI in scientific activities in Vietnam.

tieudeseo: PHUONG NAM INSTITUTE SIGNED A COOPERATION AGREEMENT ON TRAINING AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT WITH DONG THAP UNIVERSITY
mieutangan: With the aim of developing high-quality human resources and building professional training programs, Phuong Nam Institute of Social Resource Development (Phuong Nam Institute) continues to expand cooperation with educational institutions to enhance training quality and meet the development needs of society
mieutachitiet:
On January 6, 2026, in Ho Chi Minh City, Phuong Nam Institute signed a cooperation agreement with Dong Thap University.

  • In attendance at the ceremony, representatives of Dong Thap University included: Assoc. Prof. Ho Sy Thang - Vice President of Dong Thap University; together with distinguished faculty members who are representatives of the university's leadership units. 
  • Representing Phuong Nam Institute were Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute, together with distinguished faculty members who are representatives of Scientific Advisory Council and the departments/units/centers of Phuong Nam Institute.

In particular, the ceremony was honored by the presence of Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan - Director of the Medical Technology Center; Distinguished Professor of University of Technology Sydney (UTS).

The Cooperation Signing Ceremony between Dong Thap University and Phuong Nam Institute.

After a period of discussions and mutual agreement, the two institutions reached a high level of consensus on the areas of cooperation. Representatives of both parties proceeded to sign a Memorandum of Understanding on training and professional development. The cooperation covers consultancy in student recruitment for training and professional development programs at various levels and in different formats, as well as coordination in the implementation of scientific and technological activities. 

Assoc. Prof. Ho Sy Thang - The Vice President of Dong Thap University and Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute signed the cooperation agreement.

Leaders of the two institutions exchanged commemorative gifts at the ceremony.

Within the framework of the ceremony, Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan shared: When listening to your sharing about the cooperation with Phuong Nam Institute, and as I myself have also been involved in teaching in several collaborative programs with the Institute in recent times, I feel deeply engaged and appreciative. I hope that in the near future, I will have the opportunity to return to Dong Thap to further share some experiences in scientific research, especially in the context of the rapid development of artificial intelligence, through classes such as today’s program. I hope that today’s meeting and signing ceremony will open up many practical cooperation programs in the future. At the same time, I also hope that I will continue to have good health so that I can remain involved and contribute to cooperative activities and knowledge sharing with you all. Once again, I am very pleased to meet my colleagues here.

Prof. Nguyen Van Tuan delivered remarks at the cooperation signing ceremony.

The signing of the Memorandum of Understanding demonstrates the proactive spirit and strong commitment of both institutions to promoting cooperation in training, research, and the development of high-quality human resources. The clearly defined agreed contents will create favorable conditions for the two institutions to work together in activities that deliver value to the community and society.

With the orientation of “Serving by Trusted Values”, Phuong Nam Institute has consistently focused on developing highly applied training and research programs aimed at sustainable goals. This cooperation signing ceremony has contributed to leveraging the strengths of both parties, marking a new stage of development and further enhancing efforts toward the effective implementation of training activities, scientific research, knowledge transfer, and community service.

tieude: Closing Ceremony of the Expert Course “Applications of AI in Data Analysis”: A Bridge to International Knowledge
mieutangan: (HCMC) – On the afternoon of January 12, 2026, Phuong Nam Institute formally held the Closing Ceremony for the Expert Course “Applications of AI in Data Analysis”.
mieutachitiet:
Running from January 6 to January 12, 2026, the course provided the research community with the latest updates on applying AI in scientific data analysis. The ceremony was attended by Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute, the organizing institution.

Notably, the training program was honored to be directly led by a team of prestigious scientists currently working in Australia:

  • Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen, PhD: Distinguished Professor at the University of Technology Sydney (UTS) – Australia and ranked in the top 0.05% of scientists worldwide in the fields of osteoporosis, bone density, and bone minerals, according to ScholarGPS (2025).
  • Thach S. Tran, PhD: Senior Research Officer at the Garvan Institute of Medical Research (Australia).
  • Huy G. Nguyen, PhD: Garvan Institute of Medical Research (Australia).

Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen and Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy alongside participants at the ceremony.

Impressive Scale and International Reach

The recent expert course on “Applications of AI in Data Analysis” recorded impressive milestones regarding its organizational scale. The program gathered nearly 150 participants, including experts and high-quality personnel from 92 diverse units, comprising educational institutions, hospitals, research institutes, and enterprises.

Specifically, the course's influence reached beyond 19 provinces and cities across the country to reach an international level. The participation of PhD candidates and practitioners working in developed nations such as the USA, Japan, Australia, and Denmark, among others, created a diverse and high-quality academic environment.

The course attracted a large number of participants from 92 domestic and international units.

Mastering Technology - Elevating Research

Through 22 in-depth lectures and the dedicated guidance of Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen and his associates, participants were equipped with a comprehensive knowledge base to meet international standards of modern scientific research.

By the end of the course, participants had fully mastered the core objectives:

  • Developing data analysis thinking and skills by combining Statistics and Artificial Intelligence (AI).
  • Proficiently using R language and RStudio software for professional analysis tasks.
  • Mastering the methods for building and evaluating prediction models.
  • Mastering Meta-Analysis techniques to synthesize scientific evidence – a "golden" skill in evidence-based medicine.
  • Applying Propensity Score/Analysis to solve causal analysis problems in real-world data.
  • Understanding and applying the Mendelian Randomization method in causal inference.

Participants receive completion certificates from Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen

Speaking at the closing ceremony, Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of Phuong Nam Institute, congratulated the participants on completing the course and expressed her hope that the knowledge gained would serve as a powerful tool to support their future research work and international publications.

Information on the expert course "Research Design and Research Ethics" taught directly by Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen (from January 23, 2026, to January 25, 2026): https://en.vienphuongnam.com.vn/the-expert-course-research-design-and-ethics/

tieude: OPENING CEREMONY OF THE EXPERT COURSE “RESEARCH DESIGN AND ETHICS”: OVER 110 PARTICIPANTS COMMITTED TO SCIENTIFIC INTEGRITY AND ELEVATING PUBLICATION QUALITY
mieutangan: (Ho Chi Minh City) – On the morning of January 23, 2026, the expert course 'Research Design and Ethics' officially commenced. By convening over 110 participants from 82 educational institutions, healthcare facilities, research institutes, and corporate entities across 19 provinces and cities, alongside international delegates, the program contributes significantly to the national endeavor of elevating research standards in Vietnam.
mieutachitiet:
Navigating Critical Challenges: From Initial Concept to International Publication

Speaking at the event, Ms. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy – Director of the Phuong Nam Institute – emphasized that the course's objective extends beyond providing theoretical knowledge; it aims to equip participants with the practical competency to independently design valid and high-quality studies from the outset. In the context of data proliferation and increasing publication pressure, elevating research to international standards requires a solid methodological "foundation" and a robust ethical "framework."

Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy delivering the opening speech.

Spanning three days (January 23–25, 2026), the course focuses on resolving critical challenges faced by researchers: from identifying and controlling sources of bias and mitigating errors that lead to rejection, to mastering ethical principles to avoid the pitfalls of "predatory" journals.

Facilitated by Distinguished Experts

The academic caliber of the course is guaranteed by the participation of two renowned speakers:

  • Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen: Distinguished Professor at the University of Technology Sydney (UTS), Australia. He is ranked within the top 0.05% of scientists worldwide in the fields of osteoporosis, bone density, and bone minerals (ScholarGPS, 2025).
  • Dr. Tran Son Thach: Senior Research Officer at the Garvan Institute of Medical Research, Australia.

Comprehensive and Rigorous Curriculum

Comprising 12 intensive lectures, the course systematically guides participants through the entire scientific research lifecycle:

  • Conceptual Foundations: Participants are instructed on formulating "robust and valid" research questions adhering to FINER criteria, mastering the progression from initial concepts to significant findings.
  • Methodological Tools: Strategies for designing standardized questionnaires, calculating sample sizes for resource optimization, and applying data analysis techniques (descriptive and inferential) to prevent "p-hacking".
  • Research Ethics and Publication: An in-depth analysis of over 50 types of bias, ethical concerns in publication such as plagiarism and honorary authorship, and case studies drawn from real-world scientific misconduct.

Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen presenting at the ceremony.

Fostering a Culture of International Standard Science

The course attracted a diverse cohort, including master's students, PhD candidates, physicians, lecturers, medical professionals, and researchers.

Panoramic view of the ceremony. Prof. Tuan V. Nguyen and Mrs. Nguyen Thi Xuan Vy with participants.

The convergence of over 110 participants from 82 diverse institutions across 19 provinces and cities, augmented by delegates from the US, Australia, Denmark, and beyond, underscores the critical demand for standardizing research capabilities in Vietnam. This course serves as a pivotal catalyst for enhancing the quality of research proposals, data reliability, and publication capacity, with the ultimate goal of fostering a rigorous, transparent, and globally integrated academic ecosystem.

tieude: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives – Vietnam (2026)
mieutangan: The Canada Fund for Local Initiatives – Vietnam (2026)
mieutachitiet:
Program/Opportunity overview

The CFLI is a program designed to support small-scale, high-impact projects in developing countries, which align with Global Affairs Canada’s thematic priority areas for engagement. The program is directed at projects conceived and designed predominantly by local partners. Projects are selected and approved by the relevant Canadian embassy or high commission. The CFLI also serves to support positive bilateral relations between Canada and recipient countries and their civil societies, by deepening contacts and supporting local endeavours.

The majority of CFLI funding is to be directed toward local civil society organizations (including non-governmental organizations) and other institutions working at the local level. Other entities, such as international, intergovernmental, multilateral and regional organizations may be eligible for funding, provided they are working with local partners and on local projects that are consistent with the objectives of the CFLI. Similarly, municipal, regional government institutions may receive funding, provided that their projects are essentially local in nature. The CFLI is always looking to fund innovative projects that deliver measurable results.

Eligible recipients include:

  • Local non-governmental, community and not-for-profit organizations,
  • Local academic institutions working on local projects,
  • International non-governmental organizations working on local development activities,
  • Intergovernmental, multilateral and regional institutions, organizations and agencies working on local development activities,
  • Municipal, regional or national government institutions or agencies of the recipient country working on local projects, and
  • Canadian non-governmental and not-for-profit organizations that are working on local development activities.

The average CFLI contribution is $20,000 to $40,000 Canadian Dollars (CAD$100,000 is the maximum allocation amount for a project eligible under CFLI). Consult the currency converter for local currency equivalents. Please note that all contributions are made in Canadian dollars and then converted to the local currency.

Application process 

  • Applications will be assessed on their merits by a selection committee.
  • Only those proposals using the designated application form and including the required budget document will be considered.
  • Due to the high volume of applications, only successful applicants will be contacted.
  • Project applications are kept on file at the embassy in accordance with Global Affairs Canada’s information management policies.

Application guidelines 

  • Proposals must be completed in English or French.
  • All projects must align with at least 1 of the following thematic priorities:
    • Inclusive governance;
    • Peace and security;
    • Growth that works for everyone;
    • Gender equality and the empowerment of women and girls in all their diversity;
    • Environment and climate action.
  • Projects spanning 2 fiscal years (April 2026 to February 2028) may be permitted depending on the project objectives and the complexity of activities.
  • All projects will be required to be completed between the date of signature of the Contribution Agreement and February 26, 2027, for single-year project or February 25, 2028, for a two-years project.
  • To obtain an application form, please contact HANOI-CFLI@international.gc.ca. Completed applications must be submitted via email at  HANOI-CFLI@international.gc.ca.
  • The deadline for submission is April 7, 2026, at 23:59 (GMT+7). Applications submitted after this deadline will not be considered.
  • For questions about the application process, please contact HANOI-CFLI@international.gc.ca.

List of costs 

The following project costs are eligible for CFLI funding:

  • administrative costs specifically related to the project,
  • overhead costs specifically related to the project,
    • Overhead costs related to a project should not exceed 15%.Missions may, at their discretion, accept a higher threshold, based on local context; security situation; complexity of project etc.
  • capital and/or operating expenditures related to the lease and/or purchase and/or building of infrastructure (for example, office rent specifically relating to the project)
  • installation, maintenance, shipping and/or transportation costs, including fuel
  • computers and communication devices
  • internet costs specifically relating to the project
  • salary costs relating to the project
  • civic education costs
  • conference and event expenditures
  • hospitality costs, excluding alcoholic beverages
  • training and capacity building expenditures
  • costs of services received by recipients
  • outreach, communication and information dissemination costs
  • environmental assessment costs
  • advocacy and lobbying related costs
  • legal costs
  • accounting costs
  • medical costs
  • publishing costs
  • radio and television broadcast fees
  • facilities and equipment rental charges
  • research-related costs
  • security costs
  • translation and interpretation fees
  • travel expenses, using lowest fares possible but not exceeding full fare economy class
  • lease or rental of vehicles
  • vehicle and equipment operation, installation and/or maintenance
  • website development and related costs
  • miscellaneous expenses integral to the project

The following project costs are not eligible for CFLI funding:

  • purchase of vehicle
  • nuclear technologies and facilities
  • assistance to military or paramilitary organizations
  • gifts
  • luxury goods
  • direct fiscal support to a government
  • core funding or recurrent costs of an organization
  • revolving funds, (that is, funds used to advance loans to individuals, such as microfinance)
  • expenses incurred prior to the signing of the CA, or after it expires
